visa_free.nationality: EU/EEA Member State nationals (all EU member states, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway)
visa_free.notes: No visa or residence permit required; freedom of movement applies. EU citizens registered after 3 months.
No visa required; bilateral agreement with Schengen/EU freedom of movement applies.

visa_free.nationality: Schengen Annex II third countries (visa-exempt for up to 90 days in any 180-day period)
visa_free.notes: Finland applies EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II (consolidated version as of 30 December 2025, incorporating amendment Regulation EU 2025/2441 effective 30 December 2025; prior amendment M6 Regulation EU 2025/11 effective 19 December 2024 per consolidated version 03.02.2025). Nationals of these countries do not need a visa for stays up to 90 days in any 180-day period. Includes: Albania (biometric passport only), Andorra, Antigua and Barbuda, Argentina, Australia, Bahamas, Barbados, Bosnia and Herzegovina (biometric passport only), Brazil, Brunei, Canada, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Dominica, El Salvador, Georgia (biometric ordinary passport only - NOTE: diplomatic/service/official passports suspended 6 March 2026 to 6 March 2027 per Commission Implementing Regulation EU 2026/496), Grenada, Guatemala, Honduras, Hong Kong SAR (SAR passport only), Israel, Japan, Kiribati, Kosovo (biometric passport only - visa-free since 1 January 2024), Macau SAR (SAR passport only), Malaysia, Marshall Islands, Mauritius, Mexico, Micronesia, Moldova (biometric passport only), Monaco, Montenegro (biometric passport only), Nauru, New Zealand, Nicaragua, North Macedonia (biometric passport only), Palau, Panama, Paraguay, Peru, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Samoa, San Marino, Serbia (biometric passport only, excluding Serbian Coordination Directorate passports), Seychelles, Singapore, Solomon Islands, South Korea, Taiwan (passport with identity card number), Timor-Leste, Tonga, Trinidad and Tobago, Tuvalu, Ukraine (biometric passport only), United Arab Emirates, United Kingdom, United States, Uruguay, Vatican City. NOTE: Vanuatu was permanently moved to Annex I (visa required) by amendment M6 of Regulation 2018/1806 (effective 19 December 2024) and is NO LONGER visa-exempt.

eta.nationality: Schengen Annex II visa-exempt third country nationals (except EU/EEA/Swiss citizens)
eta.notes: ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) will be required for visa-exempt third country nationals to visit Finland and the Schengen Area. ETIAS is expected to launch in Q4 2026. The fee is EUR 20 per application (increased from the originally proposed EUR 7). EU/EEA/Swiss citizens and holders of valid Schengen residence permits, Schengen long-stay (Type D) visas, or Schengen short-stay (Type C) visas are exempt from ETIAS. ETIAS does not permit work or study. Dual nationals who are EU citizens must use their EU passport to enter the Schengen Area once ETIAS is operational.

visa_policy.notes: Finland is a Schengen member state and applies EU Regulation 2018/1806 for visa policy. Finland only grants Schengen Category C visas (max 90 days). Category A airport transit visas are required for nationals of: Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Democratic Republic of Congo, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Ghana, Iran, Iraq, Nigeria, Pakistan, Somalia, and Sri Lanka (confirmed via um.fi/faq-visas-and-residence-permits). Russia: citizens may enter only in exempted categories (family of EU/EEA residents, work visa holders, students, etc.). There is no visa-on-arrival or e-visa scheme; Finland does not offer electronic visas. Individual country visa requirement pages are listed at um.fi/visa-requirement-and-travel-documents-accepted-by-finland. VANUATU: removed from Annex II (visa-free list) and moved to Annex I (visa required) effective 19 December 2024 per Regulation EU 2025/11 / amendment M6 to Regulation 2018/1806. EL SALVADOR and SINGAPORE: added to Annex II notes (2026-06-27 validation pass) - both confirmed in the December 2025 consolidated text (02018R1806-20251230) of Regulation 2018/1806.

credential.subtype: any valid Schengen residence permit (issued by any Schengen member state)

conditional_access.conditions: A third-country national holding a valid residence permit issued by any Schengen member state satisfies the visa entry condition for Finland under Schengen Borders Code Article 6(1)(b) (Regulation EU 2016/399): they do not need an additional Finnish or Schengen short-stay visa. Stay subject to the 90/180-day rule. Periods of stay under a residence permit are not counted toward the 90-day limit. Transit through other Schengen states to reach the issuing state also permitted under Article 6(5)(a). Additionally, holders of a residence permit issued by an ETIAS country are exempt from the ETIAS travel authorisation requirement (ETIAS Regulation EU 2018/1240, Article 2(2); confirmed by Finnish Border Guard FAQ at raja.fi).

credential.subtype: Type D long-stay (national) visa issued by any Schengen member state

conditional_access.conditions: A third-country national holding a valid long-stay (Type D / national) visa issued by any Schengen member state satisfies the visa entry condition for Finland under Schengen Borders Code Article 6(1)(b) (Regulation EU 2016/399): no additional Finnish or Schengen short-stay visa required. Stay subject to the 90/180-day rule. Periods under a long-stay visa are not counted toward the 90-day limit. Holders of a long-stay visa issued by an ETIAS country are also exempt from ETIAS (ETIAS Regulation EU 2018/1240, Article 2(2)).

credential.subtype: Type C short-stay (uniform Schengen) visa issued by any Schengen member state

conditional_access.conditions: A valid Schengen short-stay (Type C / uniform) visa issued by any Schengen member state is valid for travel to and entry into all Schengen states including Finland, for a maximum of 90 days in any 180-day period (Schengen Visa Code, Regulation EC 810/2009; Schengen Borders Code Regulation EU 2016/399). A single-entry visa permits one entry; double-entry two entries; multiple-entry permits multiple entries within validity. Holders of a uniform (Type C) Schengen visa are also exempt from the ETIAS travel authorisation requirement (ETIAS Regulation EU 2018/1240, Article 2(2)(e)). Source also confirmed at home-affairs.ec.europa.eu: 'A Schengen visa is generally valid for every country in the Schengen area.'

Holders of valid Chinese diplomatic passports are exempt from the short-stay visa requirement for Finland and the entire Schengen Area under the EU-China Agreement on short-stay visa waiver for holders of diplomatic passports. Agreement provisionally applied from 12 February 2016; in force 1 January 2017 (Council Decisions 2016/431 and 2016/2044). Covers diplomatic passports only; service, official, and ordinary passport holders are not covered by this agreement. Maximum stay 90 days in any 180-day period.

Holders of valid Brazilian diplomatic, service, or official passports are exempt from the short-stay visa requirement for Finland and the entire Schengen Area under the EU-Brazil Agreement on short-stay visa waiver for holders of diplomatic, service or official passports. Agreement signed 8 October 2010; in force 1 April 2011 (Council Decisions 2010/621/EU and 2011/157/EU; amended agreement published OJ L 113, 11.4.2022). Covers diplomatic, service, and official passports. Ordinary Brazilian passport holders are also visa-exempt under a separate agreement (in force 1 October 2012) and under Annex II of Regulation 2018/1806. Maximum stay 90 days in any 180-day period.

Holders of Georgian diplomatic, service, or official passports have their visa exemption temporarily suspended from 6 March 2026 to 6 March 2027 under Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2026/496 of 6 March 2026. During this period they require a Schengen visa. Ordinary biometric passport holders from Georgia remain visa-exempt under Annex II of Regulation 2018/1806 (biometric passports only). The suspension may be extended by a further 24 months and could be expanded to all Georgian nationals if the underlying governance concerns are not addressed.

cbi.notes: Finland has no citizenship-by-investment program. Finnish authorities have supported EU-wide efforts to curb sale of citizenship. The only path to citizenship is through naturalisation after fulfilling residency, language, and integration requirements. As of 1 October 2024, the general requirement is 8 years of residence; most applicants can apply after 5 years if language skills are met. Source: https://migri.fi/en/citizenship-for-adults and https://migri.fi/en/faq-finnish-citizenship

programs.name: Entrepreneur Residence Permit
programs.notes: No minimum investment threshold. Applicant must actively work in the company in Finland (not just own it). Business must be profitable and income must be sufficient for livelihood. Financial resources assessed by the Centre for Economic Development, Transport and the Environment (ELY Centre/elinvoimakeskus.fi). Monthly net income requirement: at least EUR 1,030–1,210 (2026, depending on region). No pure real estate investment pathway. UPDATED 8 January 2026: General permanent residence permit path is now 6 years (was 4). However, an entrepreneur with annual income over EUR 40,000 may qualify after 4 years, or after 4 years with 3 years work history and C1 language skills. Citizenship after approximately 5 years with language skills (citizenship rules unchanged).

programs.name: Start-up Entrepreneur Residence Permit
programs.notes: For founders of growth-oriented startup companies. Requires positive Eligibility Statement from Business Finland (average processing ~1 month). First permit granted for 2 years. Fast-track option available: permit issued in 2 weeks. Monthly net income requirement: at least EUR 1,030–1,210 (2026). No stated minimum investment amount by Migri; Business Finland assesses viability of the startup. Only available as first permit (not extension); cannot apply if already in Finland. UPDATED 8 January 2026: General permanent residence permit path is now 6 years (was 4). Alternative 4-year paths exist for high-income (EUR 40,000/year) or high language (C1) with 3 years work history applicants.

programs.name: Fast Track for Specialist
programs.eligibility: Coming to Finland for expert duties requiring special expertise; must have higher education degree or equivalent expertise through work experience; confirmed employment; salary at least EUR 3,937/month (2026); first residence permit only (not extension); must not already be in Finland
programs.notes: Salary threshold confirmed at EUR 3,937/month for 2026 per migri.fi/en/income-requirement. Fringe benefits not counted. Employer must add terms of employment within 2 working days via Enter Finland for Employers. Application fee (online): EUR 530 (first permit). Permit valid up to 2 years.

programs.name: Fast Track for EU Blue Card
programs.eligibility: Highly qualified employment; higher education degree (minimum 3 years) or 5 years professional experience at equivalent level; employment contract minimum 6 months; salary at least EUR 3,937/month (2026); first residence permit only
programs.notes: EU Blue Card valid maximum 2 years (or duration of employment + 3 months, whichever shorter). Salary threshold confirmed at EUR 3,937/month for 2026 per migri.fi (same threshold as Specialist). No labour market test required. Application fee online: EUR 380 (per EC immigration portal). D visa can be applied simultaneously for immediate travel.

programs.name: Fast Track for Startup Entrepreneur
programs.eligibility: Founder or co-founder of growth-oriented startup; must have positive Eligibility Statement from Business Finland (valid max 4 months at time of application); first residence permit only; cannot be in Finland already
programs.notes: Fast track not possible if identity not proven within 5 working days, if further information is needed, or if requirements are not met. Business Finland eligibility statement processing ~1 month average. Permit valid 2 years.

programs.name: Fast Track for Specialist and Manager (ICT)
programs.eligibility: Intra-corporate transferees (specialists and managers within a multinational company); confirmed assignment in Finland
programs.notes: Part of Finland's fast track service for work-based residence permits. First permit only.

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Visa (Type C) - Tourist/Visit
visa_types.purpose: Short-term visits for tourism, recreation, or private purposes to Finland and the Schengen Area for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
visa_types.notes: Standard Schengen visa fee EUR 80 (adults) as of 2019 amendment to Visa Code (Regulation EU 2019/1155). Children aged 6–17 are exempt. Multiple, double, or single entry possible depending on travel history. Applications must be submitted in person at Finnish diplomatic mission or authorized visa centre. Processing normally 15 calendar days, up to 30–60 days in complex cases. Single-entry visa validity fixed; multiple-entry visas can be valid up to 5 years. No e-visa or visa-on-arrival for Finland.

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Visa (Type C) - Business
visa_types.purpose: Short-term business activities in Finland including meetings, conferences, and negotiations for up to 90 days in any 180-day period. Does not permit employment.
visa_types.notes: Same Schengen Type C visa as tourist; purpose declared as business on application. Fee EUR 80 (adults). Cannot be used for paid employment in Finland. Applications must be submitted in person at Finnish diplomatic mission.

visa_types.name: Airport Transit Visa (Type A)
visa_types.purpose: Authorises transit through the international transit area of Finnish airports (no entry into Finland) for nationals of countries required to hold such a visa.
visa_types.notes: Required for nationals of: Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Democratic Republic of Congo, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Ghana, Iran, Iraq, Nigeria, Pakistan, Somalia, and Sri Lanka - even when only transiting through a Finnish airport without entering Finland. Fee is same as standard Schengen visa (EUR 80). Does not permit entry into Finnish territory.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit for an Employed Person (Työntekijän oleskelulupa)
visa_types.purpose: For non-EU/EEA nationals coming to Finland to work in occupations not covered by sector-specific permits (e.g. cooks, cleaners, nursing assistants). Requires employer sponsorship and may require a labour market test.
visa_types.notes: Applied via Enter Finland online portal; identity verified in person at Finnish diplomatic mission within 3 months. Employer submits employment terms via Enter Finland for Employers. May require TE Services labour market test to verify no available EU/EEA worker. Fee charged at application; specific EUR amount not accessible during research (migri.fi returned 403). D visa valid 100 days can be applied simultaneously for early travel. Permit valid typically up to 2 years, renewable.

visa_types.name: Specialist Residence Permit (Fast Track)
visa_types.purpose: For highly qualified non-EU/EEA specialists with special expertise, including IT specialists and higher education graduates, coming to work in Finland on employment requiring special skills.
visa_types.notes: Salary requirement minimum EUR 3,937/month (2026). First permit only; must apply from outside Finland. Online application fee EUR 530. Fast-track processing maximum 2 weeks from identity verification at Finnish mission (within 5 working days). Spouse and children may apply simultaneously. D visa valid 100 days granted simultaneously.

visa_types.name: EU Blue Card (Fast Track)
visa_types.purpose: For highly qualified non-EU/EEA professionals with a higher education degree (minimum 3 years) or 5 years equivalent professional experience, employed in Finland under a qualifying employment contract.
visa_types.notes: Salary requirement minimum EUR 3,937/month (2026). Employment contract must be at least 6 months. First permit only. No labour market test. Fee approximately EUR 380 online (per EC immigration portal). Valid up to 2 years or employment duration + 3 months, whichever shorter. EU Blue Card enables mobility to other EU states after 18 months.

visa_types.name: Intra-Corporate Transferee (ICT) Residence Permit - Specialist/Manager
visa_types.purpose: For specialists and managers transferred within a multinational company to work at a Finnish entity of the same corporate group.
visa_types.notes: Fast-track processing available (2 weeks). Governed by EU Directive 2014/66/EU (ICT Directive). First permit only. Specific fee not confirmed during research (migri.fi returned 403).

visa_types.name: Seasonal Worker's Permit/Visa
visa_types.purpose: For non-EU/EEA nationals coming to Finland for seasonal agricultural or tourism work (e.g. plant growing, forestry, festival work) for up to 9 months.
visa_types.notes: Stays under 90 days: apply for seasonal work visa at Finnish embassy/consulate. Stays 90–270 days: apply to Finnish Immigration Service (Migri) via Enter Finland. Maximum permitted stay is 9 months (270 days). Employer must apply for a seasonal work notification first via TE Services.

visa_types.name: Job Seeker / Post-Graduation Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign graduates who completed a degree in Finland to remain for up to 2 years to seek employment. Also available for those who have graduated from a Finnish institution within the last 5 years.
visa_types.notes: Must have completed a degree program in Finland. Can be applied within 5 years of student permit expiration. Valid up to 2 years. Does not require a job offer at the time of application. Once employment is obtained, must switch to a work-based residence permit.

visa_types.name: Researcher Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: For non-EU/EEA researchers coming to Finland to conduct scientific research at a Finnish research organisation under a hosting agreement.
visa_types.notes: Based on EU Directive 2016/801. Requires a hosting agreement with an approved Finnish research institution. Family members eligible for simultaneous permit. Specific fee not confirmed during research.

visa_types.name: Student Residence Permit (A - Continuous)
visa_types.purpose: For non-EU/EEA students enrolled in a higher education degree programme at an approved Finnish institution. Granted for the full duration of the degree programme.
visa_types.notes: Requires acceptance letter from approved Finnish educational institution, proof of funds (min EUR 800/month, approx EUR 9,600/year), health insurance (deductible max EUR 300), and paid tuition or scholarship proof. Applied via Enter Finland; identity verified in person at Finnish mission within 3 months. Type A permit (continuous). D visa valid 100 days may be issued simultaneously. Extensions require transcript showing adequate academic progress.

visa_types.name: Student Residence Permit (B - Temporary)
visa_types.purpose: For non-EU/EEA students on exchange programmes, supplementary training, or other non-degree study at an approved Finnish institution.
visa_types.notes: Type B permit (temporary) for exchange or short-programme students. Same financial and documentation requirements as degree-programme permit. Validity matched to study duration.

visa_types.name: Family Reunification Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows non-EU/EEA family members (spouse, minor children, and in limited cases other dependants) to join a family member residing legally in Finland.
visa_types.notes: Eligible family members: spouses (including registered partners and cohabitants of 2+ years), unmarried minor children (under 18), parents of minors in Finland. Sponsor in Finland and/or applicant often required to demonstrate sufficient income. Children whose guardian(s) hold permanent permits (P) or long-term EU resident permits (P-EU) may receive permanent permits directly. Must apply from abroad before first entry for first permit.

visa_types.name: Entrepreneur's Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: For non-EU/EEA nationals establishing or operating a business in Finland as a sole proprietor, partner in a general or limited partnership, or active owner-operator of a company.
visa_types.notes: Applicant must actively work in the company in Finland (not merely own it). Business must be profitable with income sufficient for livelihood (min approx EUR 1,030–1,210/month net, 2026). ELY Centre/elinvoimakeskus.fi assesses profitability before Migri decides on the permit. Must register business in Trade Register before applying. Cannot hold permit solely for investment/ownership.

visa_types.name: Start-up Entrepreneur's Residence Permit (Fast Track)
visa_types.purpose: For founders of growth-oriented startup companies in Finland. Requires a positive eligibility statement from Business Finland confirming the startup's growth potential.
visa_types.notes: Two-phase process: obtain positive Eligibility Statement from Business Finland (processing ~1 month; statement valid 4 months at time of application), then apply to Migri. Fast-track option: maximum 2 weeks. First permit only; cannot already be in Finland. Valid 2 years. Monthly net income requirement min EUR 1,030–1,210 (2026). D visa valid 100 days granted simultaneously.

visa_types.name: Permanent Residence Permit (P)
visa_types.purpose: Grants indefinite right of residence in Finland to non-EU/EEA nationals who have resided in Finland legally and continuously for the required period.
visa_types.notes: UPDATED 8 January 2026 (Aliens Act amendment): General requirement is now 6 years of continuous legal residence in Finland, with at least 2 years of work history in Finland and satisfactory Finnish or Swedish language skills (level B1). Alternative paths: (a) 4 years continuous residence with annual income over EUR 40,000; or (b) 4 years continuous residence with at least 3 years of work history in Finland and particularly good Finnish or Swedish language skills (level C1). Applications submitted before 8 January 2026 were assessed under the prior 4-year rule. Continuous permit (A) must have been held. Permanent permit card renewed every 5 years but permit itself does not expire. Source: migri.fi/en/amendments-to-aliens-act-regarding-permanent-residence-permits-2026 (confirmed via web search; direct migri.fi access returned 403 during this validation pass).

visa_types.name: Long-Term EU Resident Permit (P-EU)
visa_types.purpose: EU-harmonised long-term residence status for non-EU/EEA nationals who have resided legally in Finland for 5 consecutive years. Facilitates mobility to other EU member states.
visa_types.notes: Based on EU Directive 2003/109/EC (Long-Term Residents Directive). Requires 5 continuous years of legal residence. Holder may move to and work in other EU member states under simplified procedures. Card renewed every 5 years but status is permanent.

visa_types.name: Temporary Protection Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: Grants temporary protection to displaced persons who have fled a country in a mass displacement situation (e.g. war) and cannot return safely - currently applied to Ukrainian nationals.
visa_types.notes: Based on EU Temporary Protection Directive 2001/55/EC. Activated by EU Council decision. Currently in effect for Ukrainian nationals displaced since 24 February 2022. Fee-free. Granted for 1 year initially, renewable while protection is in force. Holder is entitled to accommodation, healthcare, schooling, and work in Finland.

visa_types.name: D Visa (National Long-Stay Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Allows entry into Finland while awaiting the physical residence permit card. Valid for travel to Finland so the permit card can be collected; does not in itself grant residence.
visa_types.notes: Issued simultaneously with the main residence permit application. Valid 100 days. Enables holder to travel to Finland before the residence permit card is issued and mailed. Also valid for transit in other Schengen states to reach Finland (Schengen Borders Code Art. 6). Cannot be used for extended Schengen travel - only for entry into Finland.

validation_notes: CORRECTED (2026-06-14): Vanuatu removed from Annex II visa-free list. Per consolidated Regulation 2018/1806 as of 3 February 2025 (amendment M6, Regulation EU 2025/11, effective 19 December 2024), Vanuatu was permanently transferred to Annex I (visa required). Prior record listed Vanuatu as '(suspended)' in Annex II; now fully removed from visa-free list. Note added to visa_policy.notes.

CORRECTED (2026-06-14): Georgia diplomatic/service/official passport suspension clarified and added as a separate conditional_access entry (basis=passport_type, level=visa_required) for GEO diplomatic/service/official passports. Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2026/496 of 6 March 2026 suspends visa exemption for these passport types from 6 March 2026 to 6 March 2027. Ordinary biometric Georgian passports remain visa-free under Annex II (unchanged).
CORRECTED (2026-06-14): ETIAS fee updated to EUR 20 (from originally proposed EUR 7) per official EC sources. Prior ETA entry did not mention the fee. Added fee information to ETIAS notes.

CORRECTED (2026-06-14): Kosovo clarified as visa-free since 1 January 2024 (not contingent on ETIAS launch). The regulatory condition 'whichever date comes first' between ETIAS start and 1 January 2024 resolved in favour of the 2024 date. Kosovo biometric ordinary passport holders have been visa-free since that date. Updated Annex II note.
CONFIRMED (2026-06-14): Fast Track salary thresholds at EUR 3,937/month for both Specialist and EU Blue Card (2026) - confirmed via migri.fi search results.

CONFIRMED (2026-06-14): Airport transit visa list of 12 countries (Afghanistan, Bangladesh, DRC, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Ghana, Iran, Iraq, Nigeria, Pakistan, Somalia, Sri Lanka) confirmed via um.fi/faq-visas-and-residence-permits.
CONFIRMED (2026-06-14): No visa-on-arrival or e-visa scheme for Finland - confirmed via um.fi/visa-to-visit-finland ('All visa applications must be submitted in person').
CONFIRMED (2026-06-14): No CBI program - confirmed; Finland has no citizenship-by-investment scheme.

CORRECTED (2026-06-27): Singapore (SGP) and El Salvador (SLV) added to the Annex II country list in visa_policy.visa_free[2].notes. Both confirmed present in the consolidated version of Regulation 2018/1806 as of 30 December 2025 (02018R1806-20251230, incorporating Regulation EU 2025/2441). These nationalities were omitted from the prior notes list. Both are unconditional Annex II visa-free (90 days / 180-day period). Source: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:02018R1806-20251230

UPDATED (2026-06-27): Annex II source_url updated from the February 2025 consolidated version (02018R1806-20250203) to the December 2025 consolidated version (02018R1806-20251230) - most recent available. Amendment Regulation EU 2025/2441 (10 December 2025) incorporated; no Annex II countries added or removed by that amendment (it modified the suspension mechanism in Article 8 only). Added the December 2025 URL to source_urls.

CONFIRMED (2026-06-27): Georgia suspension (diplomatic/service/official passports only, 6 March 2026–6 March 2027) re-confirmed at home-affairs.ec.europa.eu - ordinary biometric passport holders remain visa-free under Annex II. No expansion to all passport types detected.
CONFIRMED (2026-06-27): ETIAS Q4 2026 launch date re-confirmed via home-affairs.ec.europa.eu; fee EUR 20 unchanged.

CORRECTED (2026-06-29): Permanent residence permit (P) standard requirement updated from 4 years to 6 years, reflecting Aliens Act amendment effective 8 January 2026. New pathways: (a) 6 years + 2 years work history + B1 language [general path]; (b) 4 years + annual income EUR 40,000; (c) 4 years + 3 years work history + C1 language. Updated visa_types permanent residence permit notes and rbi path_to_pr_years fields from 4 to 6 (reflecting the general path). Source: migri.fi/en/amendments-to-aliens-act-regarding-permanent-residence-permits-2026 (confirmed via web search; direct migri.fi page returned 403 during validation).

CONFIRMED (2026-06-29): Georgia diplomatic/service/official passport suspension re-confirmed per Commission Implementing Regulation EU 2026/496. Ordinary passport holders remain visa-free under Annex II. No expansion to ordinary passport holders detected. Source: home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/commission-implementing-regulation-temporary-suspension-visa-exemption-georgian-nationals-holding_en and ec.europa.eu/commission/presscorner/detail/en/ip_26_564.

CONFIRMED (2026-06-29): No new Annex II countries added or removed in 2026 (no new amending regulation to Regulation 2018/1806 found beyond December 2025 consolidated version). Vanuatu remains on Annex I (visa required). All Annex II nationalities in current record confirmed accurate.

CONFIRMED (2026-06-29): Airport transit visa list of 12 countries (Afghanistan, Bangladesh, DRC, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Ghana, Iran, Iraq, Nigeria, Pakistan, Somalia, Sri Lanka) re-confirmed via um.fi/faq-visas-and-residence-permits.
CONFIRMED (2026-06-29): No e-visa or visa-on-arrival for Finland re-confirmed via um.fi ('All visa applications must be submitted in person').
CONFIRMED (2026-06-29): ETIAS Q4 2026 re-confirmed via travel-europe.europa.eu; fee EUR 7 for adults under 18 and over 70 exempt; EUR 7/20 fee structure confirmed unchanged.
gaps: Individual country-by-country visa requirement confirmations from um.fi were not fully accessible (403 error); Annex II list sourced from EU Regulation 2018/1806 via EUR-Lex, which is the authoritative basis for Finland's visa-free policy

Permanent residence permit path: general path is now 6 years (effective 8 January 2026); alternative 4-year paths exist for high-income or high language-skill applicants; exact requirements for degree-holder routes not fully confirmed in this pass
ETIAS exact launch date not confirmed - Q4 2026 per EC sources; no specific day/month confirmed
Georgia suspension could be extended by a further 24 months or expanded to all passport types; monitoring recommended
Finland has no digital nomad visa; no investor-only (real estate, fund, bonds) residence by investment pathway
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder exemption found for Finland: no official source mentions any OCI-based or similar diaspora/special-status-based entry rule for Finland
No comprehensive list of all bilateral diplomatic passport visa waiver agreements accessible from um.fi

No conditional entry rules based on holding a US, UK, Canadian, Australian, GCC, UAE, or Japanese visa were found for Finland - Finland does not operate a visa-on-arrival or e-visa scheme based on third-country credentials
EES (Entry-Exit System) launch date and Finland implementation details not confirmed in this validation pass

migri.fi returned 403 errors during 2026-06-29 re-fetch; um.fi was accessible and key facts confirmed. Annex II and immigration rules verified via EUR-Lex official consolidated text and EC home-affairs portal

outbound_official.note: No official Finnish government outbound visa-list page located. Finland's Ministry for Foreign Affairs (um.fi) explicitly states it only grants visas for travel TO Finland and does not publish a country-by-country visa-free list for Finnish citizens traveling abroad. Finnish nationals are directed to consult destination-country missions for entry requirements. The Finnish MFA page https://um.fi/visa-and-residence-permit-finnish-citizens explicitly states: 'The Finnish Ministry for Foreign Affairs and the Finnish missions abroad only grant visas to people travelling to Finland; they do not handle visa applications of Finnish citizens travelling abroad.' No equivalent of India's mea.gov.in/vffin outbound visa-listing page exists on um.fi, finlandabroad.fi, raja.fi, or migri.fi. Rely on destination-side data.
